page: Amazon Web Services m4.2xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by the Xen hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon E5-2686 v4 processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture. The instance provides 8 dedicated vCPUs derived from 4 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. It is equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. There is no local storage or GPU hardware bundled with this instance, and it operates with a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks indicate a moderate capability profile. Multi-core CPU performance via stress-ng and PassMark CPU scores fall within the average range, as do memory bandwidth metrics, which reach 351.67 GB/sec for cached small blocks and up to 46.46 GB/sec for large block reads. However, single-core processing is relatively weak, with Geekbench single-core and single-threaded compression tasks scoring in the bottom 25%. Cryptographic performance remains average due to hardware-accelerated AES support, but database operations and Redis SET operations show weaker throughput.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the m4.2xlarge offers a stable, dedicated resource allocation suitable for legacy workloads that require a balanced memory-to-CPU ratio without the premium cost of newer generation hardware. Given the lack of local storage and moderate network baseline, it is best suited for general-purpose workloads such as static web serving, basic application hosting, and moderate multi-threaded processing tasks that do not demand high single-core speeds or local NVMe performance.
description: The Amazon Web Services m4.2xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 8 dedicated vCPUs and 32.0 GB of RAM, running on an Intel Xeon E5-2686 v4 processor with x86_64 architecture. Utilizing the Xen hypervisor, this instance offers a balanced 1:4 CPU-to-memory ratio but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Benchmark data reveals average multi-core CPU performance and memory bandwidth, alongside weaker single-core execution and database throughput. With a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps, the m4.2xlarge is qualitatively cost-effective for legacy applications and balanced workloads. It is best suited for static web serving, basic multi-threaded application hosting, and general-purpose utility tasks that do not require high-speed local NVMe storage or intensive single-threaded compute.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m4.2xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 8 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 32 GB of RAM, and a 1 Gbps network baseline, suitable for balanced legacy workloads.

tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual server with dedicated Intel Xeon processors and 32 GB of memory for stable, multi-threaded workloads.

32.0 GB of system memory providing 4.0 GB per vCPU
